name: codex_autonomous_build_research_pack
purpose: Machine-readable reading pack for building a multi-thread Codex workflow that starts from a design document and a runnable environment.

papers.title: ArchCode: Incorporating Software Requirements in Code Generation with Large Language Models
papers.best_for: Turning a design document into a structured requirement pack and testable acceptance criteria.
actionable_lessons: Extract explicit requirements from natural language descriptions.
Infer missing but necessary requirements instead of coding against a vague prompt.
Generate tests aligned to each requirement and use them to rank candidate implementations.
papers.warning: Good for requirement grounding, not a full project orchestration method by itself.

papers.title: MetaGPT: Meta Programming for A Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework
papers.best_for: Designing a role-based software delivery pipeline instead of letting all threads improvise.
actionable_lessons: Encode a standard operating procedure before launching worker threads.
Separate roles such as planning, architecture, implementation, and testing.
Use intermediate artifacts as handoff boundaries between threads.
papers.warning: Useful for orchestration design, but still needs hard execution gates and environment-aware tooling.

papers.title: Agent-Oriented Planning in Multi-Agent Systems
papers.best_for: Building a principled task graph for parallel execution.
actionable_lessons: Subtasks should be solvable, complete, and non-redundant.
Task decomposition quality directly affects downstream collaboration quality.
Parallelism without dependency control creates coordination debt.
papers.warning: Planning quality does not guarantee good code unless paired with strong repository tools and validation.

papers.title: RTADev: Intention Aligned Multi-Agent Framework for Software Development
papers.best_for: Preventing threads from drifting away from the same intent during long-running project work.
actionable_lessons: Inject alignment checks rather than assuming all threads share the same interpretation.
Use review meetings only when drift appears, not at every step.
Track intent mismatch as a first-class failure mode.
papers.warning: Alignment mechanisms add overhead; use them at explicit checkpoints.

papers.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
papers.best_for: Designing the file, search, edit, and test interfaces that coding agents need to work on real repositories.
actionable_lessons: Interface design matters as much as model quality for software engineering tasks.
Agents need structured tools for repo navigation, file edits, command execution, and testing.
Repository-level coding should be treated as interactive work, not single-shot generation.
papers.warning: Strong on execution interface design, not a full multi-thread project management recipe by itself.

papers.title: SWE-Dev: Building Software Engineering Agents with Training and Inference Scaling
papers.best_for: Learning how modern SWE agents use test synthesis and large trajectory data to improve patch quality.
actionable_lessons: Patch evaluation quality depends heavily on test quality.
Strong SWE agents need more than code generation; they need validated trajectories and test evidence.
Inference-time workflow design can materially change solve rates.
papers.warning: Strong benchmark paper, but it is still centered on issue resolution rather than full greenfield delivery.

papers.title: TDFlow: Agentic Workflows for Test Driven Development
papers.best_for: Designing a test-first execution loop with separate proposing, debugging, revision, and test-generation roles.
actionable_lessons: Force-decouple patch proposing, debugging, patch revision, and optional test generation.
Use tests as the main control signal instead of free-form self-judgment.
Treat reproduction test generation as a separate hard problem.
papers.warning: Excellent for test-driven repair, but the paper itself says reproduction test generation remains the frontier.

papers.title: CodeAgent: Autonomous Communicative Agents for Code Review
papers.best_for: Separating review from implementation and turning review into an explicit autonomous stage.
actionable_lessons: Review should check requirement alignment, defects, vulnerabilities, and maintainability.
Independent review threads catch risks that implementation threads will miss.
Review artifacts should be structured and actionable, not just free-form comments.
papers.warning: Review automation improves quality control, but cannot replace runtime validation.

papers.title: Prompting Large Language Models to Tackle the Full Software Development Lifecycle: A Case Study
papers.best_for: Reality-checking claims about end-to-end autonomous project delivery.
actionable_lessons: Do not assume good coding performance means full lifecycle competence.
Design, setup, implementation, acceptance testing, and unit testing all introduce different failure modes.
A realistic autonomous workflow needs explicit fallback and stop conditions.
papers.warning: Use this as a brake pedal: it is better at showing limits than giving a full orchestration recipe.

papers.title: OSWorld: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents for Open-Ended Tasks in Real Computer Environments
papers.best_for: Understanding how much harder real environment execution is than repository-only coding.
actionable_lessons: Treat GUI and multi-application execution as a separate capability tier.
Runtime validation should be evidence-based, not inferred from code or tests.
Computer-use benchmarks expose failures that pure coding benchmarks miss.
papers.warning: If your workflow requires real desktop or browser operations, plan for lower reliability and more guardrails.

papers.title: GitGoodBench: A Novel Benchmark For Evaluating Agentic Performance On Git
papers.best_for: Extending autonomous coding from file edits to actual Git workflows.
actionable_lessons: Version control is part of software engineering, not a side detail.
Agents need explicit competence for branching, commits, and conflict handling.
A project workflow that ignores Git operations is incomplete.
papers.warning: Workshop paper, but highly relevant if the workflow includes real repository operations.
